figures.caption: Figure 2. Complex "gene-chain" architectures at PCOS-associated genomic loci. (A170 The MTOR, PDE4B, and TNFAIP6 loci exhibit a dual configuration, characterized by171 presence of both a shared bidirectional promoter (BDP) and sense-antisense (S-AS) 172 transcript overlap. (D) The LIF locus displays a distinct '2x S-AS' architecture, involv173 multiple discontinuous regions of antisense overlap. HS: Hypothetical State (of gene174 chain expression). The symbols (+) and (−) indicate the possible expression states of t175 transcripts. Genes joined by bidirectional promoters are assumed to be coregulated. 176 Genes forming sense-antisense pairs are assumed to be capable of either positive or 177 negative regulation. Gene structures and orientations are based on UCSC Genome 178 Browser visualizations. 179
Figure 3. Screenshot of PACERR lncRNA expression in GTEx-profiled tissues. This lncRNA is 201 expressed in several key female reproductive organs and tissues, as well as elsewhere. 202

Table 3. PDEGAL lncRNA genes containing GWAS SNPs significantly associated with traits or 230 conditions directly related to PCOS. SNP identifiers, and the diseases and traits the SNPs are 231 significantly associated with, are displayed. The table also details whether these SNPs are intronic, 232 exonic, or in sense-antisense overlap regions. 233
